entry.text: A rusted steel tent frame has been wrapped in tarpaulin and crowned with a buzzing neon sign. The sign reads something that no longer makes sense. The smell hits you from twenty metres: mildew, rot, and desperation rendered into a single atmospheric event. Music of some kind is playing inside — if you're charitable with the word music. Something is moving against the fabric walls in a way that suggests rhythm but does not achieve it.

choices.label: Slip away before anyone notices you
Enter and search the tip jars while they perform

fled_safely.text: You back away into the scrub and keep walking for a good ten minutes without looking back. You are slightly traumatised by the forty-five seconds you did see. You will need to process this later.

tip_jar_loot.text: The jars are surprisingly heavy. Not everyone out here is broke. You stuff your pockets while an eight-foot mutant in a feather boa slowly and deliberately misses every beat. It would be saddening if you could look directly at it. You keep your eyes on the money.

noticed_fleeing.text: A performer spots you through the tent flap as you turn to leave. They interpret this as audience participation. You spend the next twenty minutes being enthusiastically incorporated into the act before you can escape. Your sanity takes the hit.

performance_incident.text: One of the performers sheds a limb mid-spin. It lands on you. The screaming is mutual. You run. You don't stop running for a while.
